perturbation.original_text: 2. Definitions 2.1 “Agreement” means this Confidentialit y and Non -Disclosure Agreement 2.2 "Board" means the Board of SAMED. 2.3 “Board Members” means members of the Board. 2.4 “Chair” means Chair of the Board. 2.5 "Committee" means a committee of the Board. 2.6 "Committee Members" means members of any Committe e. 2.7 "Meeting" means a meeting of the Board or a Committee, as applicable. 2.8 "Policy" means this Confidentiality Policy 2.9 “SAMED” means South African Medical Device s Industry Association 2.10 “Vice -Chair”, means Vice -Chair of the Board
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definitions 2.1 “Agreement” means this Confidentialit y and Non -Disclosure Agreement 2.2 "Board" means the Board of SAMED. 2.3 “Board Members” means members of the Board. 2.4 “Chair” means Chair of the Board. 2.5 "Working Group" means a committee of the Board. 2.6 "Working Group Members" means members of any Committe e. 2.7 "Meeting" means a meeting of the Board or a Committee, as applicable. 2.8 "Policy" means this Confidentiality Policy 2.9 “SAMED” means South African Medical Device s Industry Association 2.10 “Vice -Chair”, means Vice -Chair of the Board
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Committee' to 'Working Group' creates an in-text contradiction. Throughout the document 'Committee' is used, but the definition now refers to 'Working Group' which are not synonymous, causing confusion regarding which group the clauses refer to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.7 "Meeting" means a meeting of the Board or a Committee, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The original definition defined 'Committee' as 'a committee of the Board.' The changed text redefines 'Committee' as 'Working Group' but refers to members of any 'Committe e' which directly contradicts the contradicted text because section 2.7 uses 'Committee' but the meaning of Committee is now replaced with Working Group, creating a contradiction because the document now uses two non-synonymous terms, which causes confusion since it is no longer clear whether section 2.7 refers to a meeting of the board or a meeting of a working group.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Confidentiality 4.1 Regular Board meetings are , in the ordinary course of events, not open to the public and/or media. a) Members of SAMED may on invitation attend Board meetings. b) Once the Board has dealt with an issue in a board meeting and has decided on that matter , the issue is considered to be accessible to members to the extent of that decision. Matters that are so declared may be accessible to the public at large to the exten t so declared by the Board. c) All decisions that would be accessible to the general membership and/or the public at large will be recorded as such in the Board minutes. d) If no recordal is made, the matter, discussions and all resolutions should be deemed to be confidential unless declared by the Board by resolution as not confidential. e) When considering whether matters are confidential or not, the nature of SAMED as a membership orga nization, the prospects of harm to SAMED (including harm to its reputation, good processes and procedures and good governance), as well as the impact of any such decision on members or groups of members, must, amongst others, be considered.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Confidentiality 4.1 Regular Board meetings are , in the ordinary course of events, not open to the public and/or media. a) Members of SAMED may on invitation attend Board meetings. b) Once the Board has dealt with an issue in a board meeting and has decided on that matter , the issue is considered to be accessible to members to the extent of that decision. Matters that are so declared may be accessible to the public at large to the exten t so declared by the Board. c) All decisions that would be accessible to the general membership and/or the public at large will be recorded as such in the Directorate minutes. d) If no recordal is made, the matter, discussions and all resolutions should be deemed to be confidential unless declared by the Board by resolution as not confidential. e) When considering whether matters are confidential or not, the nature of SAMED as a membership orga nization, the prospects of harm to SAMED (including harm to its reputation, good processes and procedures and good governance), as well as the impact of any such decision on members or groups of members, must, amongst others, be considered.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Board minutes' to 'Directorate minutes' introduces confusion. It's unclear if 'Directorate' is synonymous with 'Board,' leading to uncertainty about which body's minutes are being referred to, creating contradiction with the rest of the paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b) Once the Board has dealt with an issue in a board meeting and has decided on that matter , the issue is considered to be accessible to members to the extent of that decision. Matters that are so declared may be accessible to the public at large to the exten t so declared by the Board.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Board minutes' to 'Directorate minutes' creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity. The original text and the contradicted text both refer to actions and decisions made by the 'Board'. By changing 'Board minutes' to 'Directorate minutes', it becomes unclear whether the 'Directorate' is the same as the 'Board', a sub-committee, or a completely different entity. This uncertainty directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes a clear and consistent understanding of which body is responsible for decisions and record-keeping, thus making it a strong in-text contradiction as it uses multiple terms without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Conflict of Interest 5.1 A conflict of interest arises when there is a relationship which exists which could result in the Board or Committee member being perceived as being biased either for or against the topic of discussion . 5.2 Any M ember of the SAMED Board, Executive, and/or Staff who has a personal interest, directly or indirectly, in any contract, transaction, proposed contract, or proposed transaction, under consideration of the Board of Directors or of a C ommittee created by it shall be deemed as being in a conflict of interest, where: a) Contract is defined as any written or verbal agreement between two or more parties for the doing or not doing of something specified which is related to the matter at hand. b) Transaction is defined a s an exchange of money, services, goods, position, or favours are exchanged for money, services, goods, position or favours. c) Interest is defined as any instance where any contract or transaction, proposed contract or proposed transaction could be to the b enefit or detriment of themselves, family, partners, roommates/housemates, other organizations to which they are currently affiliated, that do not pertain to their job description or mandate. 5.3 Conflicts of interest may also arise in matters that may le ad to a direct benefit to a particular Board member and/or his/her company, which position may conflict with either the law and/or codes of good practice and/or SAMED policy on a particular matter or the approach that should be taken to specific types o f matters. 5.4 Where the Board of Directors or a C ommittee created by it is of the opinion that a conflict of interest exists that has not been decla red, the Board of Directors or C ommittee may declare, by a resolution carried by two -thirds of the members present and voting at the meeting, that a conflict of interest exists and that the member found in conflict shall follow the procedure below. 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Conflict of Interest 5.1 A conflict of interest arises when there is a relationship which exists which could result in the Board or Committee member being perceived as being biased either for or against the topic of discussion . 5.2 Any M ember of the SAMED Board, Executive, and/or Staff who has a personal interest, directly or indirectly, in any contract, transaction, proposed contract, or proposed transaction, under consideration of the Board of Directors or of a Panel created by it shall be deemed as being in a conflict of interest, where: a) Contract is defined as any written or verbal agreement between two or more parties for the doing or not doing of something specified which is related to the matter at hand. b) Transaction is defined a s an exchange of money, services, goods, position, or favours are exchanged for money, services, goods, position or favours. c) Interest is defined as any instance where any contract or transaction, proposed contract or proposed transaction could be to the b enefit or detriment of themselves, family, partners, roommates/housemates, other organizations to which they are currently affiliated, that do not pertain to their job description or mandate. 5.3 Conflicts of interest may also arise in matters that may le ad to a direct benefit to a particular Board member and/or his/her company, which position may conflict with either the law and/or codes of good practice and/or SAMED policy on a particular matter or the approach that should be taken to specific types o f matters. 5.4 Where the Board of Directors or a Panel created by it is of the opinion that a conflict of interest exists that has not been decla red, the Board of Directors or C ommittee may declare, by a resolution carried by two -thirds of the members present and voting at the meeting, that a conflict of interest exists and that the member found in conflict shall follow the procedure below. 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Committee' to 'Panel' in 5.2 while maintaining 'Committee' in 5.4 creates a contradiction. It's unclear whether the rules in 5.4 apply to the 'Panel' introduced in 5.2, especially as they were originally referring to a committe
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Where the Board of Directors or a C ommittee created by it is of the opinion that a conflict of interest exists that has not been decla red, the Board of Directors or C ommittee may declare, by a resolution carried by two -thirds of the members present and voting at the meeting, that a conflict of interest exists and that the member found in conflict shall follow the procedure below.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Committee' with 'Panel' in section 5.2, while section 5.4 still refers to 'Committee'. This creates a contradiction because it's now unclear whether the procedures outlined in 5.4, which pertain to actions taken by the 'Board of Directors or a Committee,' also apply to actions taken by a 'Panel'. The original text had consistent terminology, but the change introduces ambiguity, creating an in-text contradiction as described by the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 In the event of a conflict of interest, the interested party shall: a) Prior to any vote on the issue, decl are the con flict of interest to the Chair or Board as a whole. b) Refrain from voting in relation to the matter, unless otherwise instructed by the legal counsel. c) Withdraw from the meeting when the matter is discussed if requested to do so by a simple majority of the members present and voting at the meeting.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless otherwise instructed by the legal counsel' to the obligation to refrain from voting introduces a contradiction. It undermines the integrity of the conflict of interest rule, as it allows legal counsel to override the ethical obligation. This will contradict with the original aim of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 The purpose of this agreement is to ensure that confidential matters brought before the Members of the Board of SAMED or any of its committees , are not disclosed until disclosure is properly authorized. SAMED Board and Committee members are in positions of trust towards SAMED as a vol untary membership organization, and such trust may require that elected and appointed persons act with the highest integrity and keep certain information confidential, and avoid conflicts of interest.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by adding 'unless otherwise instructed by the legal counsel' to the obligation to refrain from voting in case of a conflict of interest. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text (1.1), which states that Board members should 'act with the highest integrity and avoid conflicts of interest'. The added clause allows legal counsel to override the ethical obligation, undermining the agreement's purpose of ensuring integrity and avoiding conflicts of interest, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including a warning from the Executive Officer). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee' to 'a warning from the Executive Officer' in 6.1 while keeping the possibility of 'removal' in 6.2, creates a contradiction in the severity of consequences for a breach. It undermines the power and importance of the board.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a breach may result in 'removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee', which is a significant consequence. The changed text replaces this with 'a warning from the Executive Officer', a much less severe consequence. Section 6.2, the contradicted text, retains the possibility of 'removal', creating a direct conflict in the severity of consequences for a breach. This undermines the board's power and importance, making the consequences for breaches unclear and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, as described in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Terms 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties for the duration of one year . A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Terms 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties until further notice . A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'for the duration of one year' to 'until further notice' creates a contradiction. The original text clearly states the contract duration as one year, whereas the modification suggests an indefinite term, which are contradictory and affects enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties until further notice . A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
